question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE to force hookRepository.findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to scan the entire hook table, exhausting DB connection pool resources and causing transaction gossip processing to stall?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user flood GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks with concurrent requests using the maximum allowed limit, causing the mirror node's thread pool to saturate and preventing timely gossip of new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS hookId EQ filters simultaneously, causing the idsInRange stream filter to iterate over a maximally large list and block the service thread long enough to delay transaction gossip?
